steps.title: Spot the pivotal transition
steps.content: The passage introduces Jennie Carter's 1867 journalism, then explains that “That history is preserved by the National Association of Black Journalists, which was founded… to honor people like Carter.” This transition—from a specific historical example to an organization dedicated to preserving and honoring that history—is the key structural signal.

steps.title: Match the structure to the choice
steps.content: Choice B names the exact structure: the text mentions a specific achievement in Black journalistic history (Carter's 1867 journalism), then describes an organization dedicated to upholding that history (the NABJ, founded to honor people like Carter). The other three either misidentify the passage's content, reverse its order, or introduce unsupported elements. That matches choice B — B is correct.
